title: heracleum sphondylium var. rarum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium var. rarum, also known as the wild celery or angelica, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Heracleum sphondylium plant, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Heracleum sphondylium var. rarum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to make poultices and salves for wound healing.
